name: av

metadata.name: av
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
PyAV
====

PyAV is a Pythonic binding for the [FFmpeg][ffmpeg] libraries. We aim to provide all of the power and control of the underlying library, but manage the gritty details as much as possible.

---

[![GitHub Test Status][github-tests-badge]][github-tests] [![Documentation][docs-badge]][docs] [![Python Package Index][pypi-badge]][pypi] [![Conda Forge][conda-badge]][conda]

PyAV is for direct and precise access to your media via containers, streams, packets, codecs, and frames. It exposes a few transformations of that data, and helps you get your data to/from other packages (e.g. Numpy and Pillow).

This power does come with some responsibility as working with media is horrendously complicated and PyAV can't abstract it away or make all the best decisions for you. If the `ffmpeg` command does the job without you bending over backwards, PyAV is likely going to be more of a hindrance than a help.

But where you can't work without it, PyAV is a critical tool.


Installation
------------

Due to the complexity of the dependencies, PyAV is not always the easiest Python package to install from source. Since release 8.0.0 binary wheels are provided on [PyPI][pypi] for Linux, Mac and Windows linked against a modern FFmpeg. You can install these wheels by running:

```bash
pip install av
```

If you want to use your existing FFmpeg, the source version of PyAV is on [PyPI][pypi] too:

```bash
pip install av --no-binary av
```

Installing from source is not supported on Windows.

Alternative installation methods
--------------------------------

Another way of installing PyAV is via [conda-forge][conda-forge]:

```bash
conda install av -c conda-forge
```

See the [Conda install][conda-install] docs to get started with (mini)Conda.

And if you want to build from the absolute source (POSIX only):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/PyAV-Org/PyAV.git
cd PyAV
source scripts/activate.sh

# Build ffmpeg from source. You can skip this step
# if ffmpeg is already installed.
./scripts/build-deps

# Build PyAV
make

# Testing
make test

# Install globally
deactivate
pip install .
```

---

Have fun, [read the docs][docs], [come chat with us][discuss], and good luck!

functions.name: av.datasets.cached_download
documentation:
Download the data at a URL, and cache it under the given name.

The file is stored under `pyav/test` with the given name in the directory
{envvar}`PYAV_TESTDATA_DIR`, or the first that is writeable of:

- the current virtualenv
- `/usr/local/share`
- `/usr/local/lib`
- `/usr/share`
- `/usr/lib`
- the user's home

functions.name: av.datasets.fate
documentation:
Download and return a path to a sample from the FFmpeg test suite.

Data is handled by {func}`cached_download`.

See the [FFmpeg Automated Test Environment](https://www.ffmpeg.org/fate.html)

functions.name: av.datasets.curated
documentation:
Download and return a path to a sample that is curated by the PyAV developers.

Data is handled by {func}`cached_download`.

documentation: Buffer which can be "broken" to simulate an I/O error.

classes.name: tests.test_python_io.ReadOnlyBuffer
documentation: Minimal buffer which *only* implements the read() method.

functions.name: tests.test_encode.encode_file_with_max_b_frames
documentation:
Create an encoded video file (or file-like object) with the given
maximum run of B frames.

max_b_frames: non-negative integer which is the maximum allowed run
    of consecutive B frames.

Returns: a file-like object.

functions.name: tests.test_encode.max_b_frame_run_in_file
documentation:
Count the maximum run of B frames in a file (or file-like object).

file: the file or file-like object in which to count the maximum run
    of B frames. The file should contain just one video stream.

Returns: non-negative integer which is the maximum B frame run length.

methods.name: tests.test_encode.TestMaxBFrameEncoding.test_max_b_frames
documentation:
Test that we never get longer runs of B frames than we asked for with
the max_b_frames property.

functions.name: tests.test_audioresampler.test_flush_immediately
documentation:
If we flush the resampler before passing any input, it returns
a `None` frame without setting up the graph.

functions.name: tests.test_audioresampler.test_identity_passthrough
documentation: If we don't ask it to do anything, it won't.

functions.name: tests.test_audioresampler.test_matching_passthrough
documentation: If the frames match, it won't do anything.

functions.name: tests.test_audioresampler.test_mismatched_input
documentation: Consecutive frames must have the same layout, sample format and sample rate.
